ApiIndex
#

ApiIndex.svelte

library?

The library instance to render API docs for. Defaults to getting from library_context.

type Library
optional

tome?

The tome for the API docs page. Defaults to looking up the 'api' tome.

type { name: string; category: string; Component: Component<any, any, string>; related_tomes: string[]; related_modules: string[]; related_declarations: string[]; }
optional

minimal?

Whether to show minimal content (just a link to full API docs). Used when the API index is shown on a parent docs page.

type boolean
optional

ApiModule
#

ApiModule.svelte

module_path

The module path parameter from the route (e.g., "lib/Button.svelte").

type string | string[]

library?

The library instance to render API docs for. Defaults to getting from library_context.

type Library
optional

tome?

The tome for the API docs page. Defaults to looking up the 'api' tome.

type { name: string; category: string; Component: Component<any, any, string>; related_tomes: string[]; related_modules: string[]; related_declarations: string[]; }
optional

Breadcrumb.svelte

children?

type Snippet<[]>
optional

path?

Prefixed with a slash and relative to the base path.

type string
optional

selected_path?

Prefixed with a slash and relative to the base path. null means none and undefined is detected from the current url.

type string
optional

base_path?

Sets a custom base path of path and selected_path. Defaults to base from $app/paths.

type string
optional

separator?

type Snippet<[]>
optional

contextmenu_open

contextmenu_state.svelte.ts

target

the leaf element from which to open the contextmenu

type HTMLElement | SVGElement

x

the page X coordinate at which to open the contextmenu, typically the mouse pageX

type number

y

the page Y coordinate at which to open the contextmenu, typically the mouse pageY

type number

contextmenu

the contextmenu store

type ContextmenuState

options?

optional configuration for filtering entries and haptic feedback

type ContextmenuOpenOptions | undefined
optional

ContextmenuParams
#

contextmenu_state.svelte.ts

ContextmenuRoot
#

ContextmenuRoot.svelte

contextmenu?

type ContextmenuState
optional

open_offset_x?

The number of pixels to offset from the pointer X position when opened. Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

type number
optional

open_offset_y?

The number of pixels to offset from the pointer Y position when opened. Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

type number
optional

bypass_with_tap_then_longpress?

Whether to detect tap-then-longpress to bypass the Fuz contextmenu. This allows access to the system contextmenu by tapping once then rightclicking/long-pressing. Setting to false disables the gesture.

type boolean
optional

bypass_window?

The number of milliseconds between taps to detect a gesture that bypasses the Fuz contextmenu. Used only when bypass_with_tap_then_longpress is true. If the duration is too long, it'll detect more false positives and interrupt normal usage, but too short and some people will have difficulty performing the gesture.

type number
optional

bypass_move_tolerance?

The number of pixels the pointer can be moved between taps to detect a tap-then-longpress. Used only when bypass_with_tap_then_longpress is true.

type number
optional

scoped?

If true, wraps children with a div and listens to events on it instead of the window.

type boolean
optional

link_entry?

Snippet for rendering link entries. Set to null to disable automatic link detection. Defaults to link_entry_default which renders ContextmenuLinkEntry.

type Snippet<[any]>
optional

text_entry?

Snippet for rendering copy text entries. Set to null to disable automatic copy text detection. Defaults to text_entry_default which renders ContextmenuTextEntry.

type Snippet<[any]>
optional

separator_entry?

Snippet for rendering separator entries. Set to null to disable automatic separator rendering. Defaults to separator_entry_default which renders ContextmenuSeparator.

type Snippet<[any]>
optional

children

type Snippet<[]>

ContextmenuRootForSafariCompatibility
#

ContextmenuRootForSafariCompatibility.svelte

contextmenu?

type ContextmenuState
optional

longpress_move_tolerance?

The number of pixels the pointer can be moved without canceling longpress.

type number
optional

longpress_duration?

The number of milliseconds after a touch starts before opening the Fuz contextmenu.

type number
optional

bypass_with_tap_then_longpress?

Whether to detect tap-then-longpress to bypass the Fuz contextmenu. This allows access to the system contextmenu by tapping once then long-pressing. Setting to false disables the gesture.

type boolean
optional

bypass_window?

The number of milliseconds between taps to detect a gesture that bypasses the Fuz contextmenu. Used only when bypass_with_tap_then_longpress is true. If the duration is too long, it'll detect more false positives and interrupt normal usage, but too short and some people will have difficulty performing the gesture.

type number
optional

bypass_move_tolerance?

The number of pixels the pointer can be moved between taps to detect a tap-then-longpress. Used only when bypass_with_tap_then_longpress is true.

type number
optional

open_offset_x?

The number of pixels to offset from the pointer X position when opened. Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

type number
optional

open_offset_y?

The number of pixels to offset from the pointer Y position when opened. Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

type number
optional

scoped?

If true, wraps children with a div and listens to events on it instead of the window.

type boolean
optional

link_entry?

Snippet for rendering link entries. Set to null to disable automatic link detection. Defaults to link_entry_default which renders ContextmenuLinkEntry.

type Snippet<[any]>
optional

text_entry?

Snippet for rendering copy text entries. Set to null to disable automatic copy text detection. Defaults to text_entry_default which renders ContextmenuTextEntry.

type Snippet<[any]>
optional

separator_entry?

Snippet for rendering separator entries. Set to null to disable automatic separator rendering. Defaults to separator_entry_default which renders ContextmenuSeparator.

type Snippet<[any]>
optional

children

type Snippet<[]>

inputmode?

Hints at the type of data that might be entered by the user while editing the element or its contents

type "none" | "search" | "text" | "tel" | "url" | "email" | "numeric" | "decimal"
optional

is?

Specify that a standard HTML element should behave like a defined custom built-in element

type string
optional

bind:innerHTML?

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

type string
optional

bind:textContent?

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

type string
optional

bind:innerText?

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

type string
optional

bind:focused?

optional

bind:offsetWidth?

bind:offsetHeight?

type number

data-sveltekit-keepfocus?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-noscroll?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-preload-code?

type true | "" | "off" | "eager" | "viewport" | "hover" | "tap"
optional

data-sveltekit-preload-data?

type true | "" | "off" | "hover" | "tap"
optional

data-sveltekit-reload?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-replacestate?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

aria-activedescendant?

Identifies the currently active element when DOM focus is on a composite widget, textbox, group, or application.

type string
optional

aria-atomic?

Indicates whether assistive technologies will present all, or only parts of, the changed region based on the change notifications defined by the aria-relevant attribute.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-autocomplete?

Indicates whether inputting text could trigger display of one or more predictions of the user's intended value for an input and specifies how predictions would be presented if they are made.

type "none" | "list" | "inline" | "both"
optional

aria-busy?

Indicates an element is being modified and that assistive technologies MAY want to wait until the modifications are complete before exposing them to the user.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-checked?

Indicates the current "checked" state of checkboxes, radio buttons, and other widgets.

type boolean | "true" | "false" | "mixed"
optional

aria-colcount?

Defines the total number of columns in a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-colindex?

Defines an element's column index or position with respect to the total number of columns within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-colspan?

Defines the number of columns spanned by a cell or gridcell within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-controls?

Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current element.

type string
optional

aria-current?

Indicates the element that represents the current item within a container or set of related elements.

type Booleanish | "date" | "page" | "step" | "location" | "time"
optional

aria-describedby?

Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

type string
optional

aria-details?

Identifies the element that provides a detailed, extended description for the object.

type string
optional

aria-disabled?

Indicates that the element is perceivable but disabled, so it is not editable or otherwise operable.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-dropeffect?

Indicates what functions can be performed when a dragged object is released on the drop target.

type "none" | "link" | "copy" | "execute" | "move" | "popup"
optional

aria-errormessage?

Identifies the element that provides an error message for the object.

type string
optional

aria-expanded?

Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded or collapsed.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-flowto?

Identifies the next element (or elements) in an alternate reading order of content which, at the user's discretion, allows assistive technology to override the general default of reading in document source order.

type string
optional

aria-grabbed?

Indicates an element's "grabbed" state in a drag-and-drop operation.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-haspopup?

Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that can be triggered by an element.

type Booleanish | "dialog" | "grid" | "listbox" | "menu" | "tree"
optional

aria-hidden?

Indicates whether the element is exposed to an accessibility API.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-invalid?

Indicates the entered value does not conform to the format expected by the application.

type Booleanish | "grammar" | "spelling"
optional

aria-keyshortcuts?

Indicates keyboard shortcuts that an author has implemented to activate or give focus to an element.

type string
optional

aria-label?

Defines a string value that labels the current element.

type string
optional

aria-labelledby?

Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

type string
optional

aria-level?

Defines the hierarchical level of an element within a structure.

type number
optional

aria-live?

Indicates that an element will be updated, and describes the types of updates the user agents, assistive technologies, and user can expect from the live region.

type "off" | "assertive" | "polite"
optional

aria-modal?

Indicates whether an element is modal when displayed.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-multiline?

Indicates whether a text box accepts multiple lines of input or only a single line.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-multiselectable?

Indicates that the user may select more than one item from the current selectable descendants.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-orientation?

Indicates whether the element's orientation is horizontal, vertical, or unknown/ambiguous.

type "horizontal" | "vertical"
optional

aria-owns?

Identifies an element (or elements) in order to define a visual, functional, or contextual parent/child relationship between DOM elements where the DOM hierarchy cannot be used to represent the relationship.

type string
optional

aria-placeholder?

Defines a short hint (a word or short phrase) intended to aid the user with data entry when the control has no value. A hint could be a sample value or a brief description of the expected format.

type string
optional

aria-posinset?

Defines an element's number or position in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.

type number
optional

aria-pressed?

Indicates the current "pressed" state of toggle buttons.

type boolean | "true" | "false" | "mixed"
optional

aria-readonly?

Indicates that the element is not editable, but is otherwise operable.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-relevant?

Indicates what notifications the user agent will trigger when the accessibility tree within a live region is modified.

type "text" | "additions" | "additions removals" | "additions text" | "all" | "removals" | "removals additions" | "removals text" | "text additions" | "text removals"
optional

aria-required?

Indicates that user input is required on the element before a form may be submitted.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-roledescription?

Defines a human-readable, author-localized description for the role of an element.

type string
optional

aria-rowcount?

Defines the total number of rows in a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-rowindex?

Defines an element's row index or position with respect to the total number of rows within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-rowspan?

Defines the number of rows spanned by a cell or gridcell within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-selected?

Indicates the current "selected" state of various widgets.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-setsize?

Defines the number of items in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.

type number
optional

aria-sort?

Indicates if items in a table or grid are sorted in ascending or descending order.

type "none" | "ascending" | "descending" | "other"
optional

aria-valuemax?

Defines the maximum allowed value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuemin?

Defines the minimum allowed value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuenow?

Defines the current value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuetext?

Defines the human readable text alternative of aria-valuenow for a range widget.

type string
optional

children?

type Snippet<[]>
optional

Details
#

Details.svelte

open?

type boolean
optional bindable

children

type Snippet<[]>

eager?

Children are lazily rendered by default, enabling transitions and improving performance, sometimes significantly. Pass true to render children eagerly to gain ctrl+f functionality while trading away the benefits. Consider a <details> element instead of making this component eager.

type boolean
optional

summary

type string | Snippet<[]>

summary_attrs?

type HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>
optional

Dialog
#

Dialog.svelte

container?

type HTMLElement
optional

layout?

type DialogLayout
optional

index?

index 0 is under 1 is under 2 etc -- the topmost dialog is the last in the array

type number
optional

active?

type boolean
optional

content_selector?

If provided, prevents clicks that would close the dialog from bubbling past any elements matching this selector.

type string
optional

onclose?

type () => void
optional

children

type Snippet<[close: (e?: Event | undefined) => void]>

type string

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-noscroll?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-preload-code?

type true | "" | "off" | "eager" | "viewport" | "hover" | "tap"
optional

data-sveltekit-preload-data?

type true | "" | "off" | "hover" | "tap"
optional

data-sveltekit-reload?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-replacestate?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

aria-activedescendant?

Identifies the currently active element when DOM focus is on a composite widget, textbox, group, or application.

type string
optional

aria-atomic?

Indicates whether assistive technologies will present all, or only parts of, the changed region based on the change notifications defined by the aria-relevant attribute.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-autocomplete?

Indicates whether inputting text could trigger display of one or more predictions of the user's intended value for an input and specifies how predictions would be presented if they are made.

type "none" | "list" | "inline" | "both"
optional

aria-busy?

Indicates an element is being modified and that assistive technologies MAY want to wait until the modifications are complete before exposing them to the user.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-checked?

Indicates the current "checked" state of checkboxes, radio buttons, and other widgets.

type boolean | "true" | "false" | "mixed"
optional

aria-colcount?

Defines the total number of columns in a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-colindex?

Defines an element's column index or position with respect to the total number of columns within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-colspan?

Defines the number of columns spanned by a cell or gridcell within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-controls?

Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current element.

type string
optional

aria-current?

Indicates the element that represents the current item within a container or set of related elements.

type Booleanish | "date" | "page" | "step" | "location" | "time"
optional

aria-describedby?

Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

type string
optional

aria-details?

Identifies the element that provides a detailed, extended description for the object.

type string
optional

aria-disabled?

Indicates that the element is perceivable but disabled, so it is not editable or otherwise operable.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-dropeffect?

Indicates what functions can be performed when a dragged object is released on the drop target.

type "none" | "link" | "copy" | "execute" | "move" | "popup"
optional

aria-errormessage?

Identifies the element that provides an error message for the object.

type string
optional

aria-expanded?

Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded or collapsed.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-flowto?

Identifies the next element (or elements) in an alternate reading order of content which, at the user's discretion, allows assistive technology to override the general default of reading in document source order.

type string
optional

aria-grabbed?

Indicates an element's "grabbed" state in a drag-and-drop operation.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-haspopup?

Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that can be triggered by an element.

type Booleanish | "dialog" | "grid" | "listbox" | "menu" | "tree"
optional

aria-hidden?

Indicates whether the element is exposed to an accessibility API.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-invalid?

Indicates the entered value does not conform to the format expected by the application.

type Booleanish | "grammar" | "spelling"
optional

aria-keyshortcuts?

Indicates keyboard shortcuts that an author has implemented to activate or give focus to an element.

type string
optional

aria-label?

Defines a string value that labels the current element.

type string
optional

aria-labelledby?

Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

type string
optional

aria-level?

Defines the hierarchical level of an element within a structure.

type number
optional

aria-live?

Indicates that an element will be updated, and describes the types of updates the user agents, assistive technologies, and user can expect from the live region.

type "off" | "assertive" | "polite"
optional

aria-modal?

Indicates whether an element is modal when displayed.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-multiline?

Indicates whether a text box accepts multiple lines of input or only a single line.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-multiselectable?

Indicates that the user may select more than one item from the current selectable descendants.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-orientation?

Indicates whether the element's orientation is horizontal, vertical, or unknown/ambiguous.

type "horizontal" | "vertical"
optional

aria-owns?

Identifies an element (or elements) in order to define a visual, functional, or contextual parent/child relationship between DOM elements where the DOM hierarchy cannot be used to represent the relationship.

type string
optional

aria-placeholder?

Defines a short hint (a word or short phrase) intended to aid the user with data entry when the control has no value. A hint could be a sample value or a brief description of the expected format.

type string
optional

aria-posinset?

Defines an element's number or position in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.

type number
optional

aria-pressed?

Indicates the current "pressed" state of toggle buttons.

type boolean | "true" | "false" | "mixed"
optional

aria-readonly?

Indicates that the element is not editable, but is otherwise operable.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-relevant?

Indicates what notifications the user agent will trigger when the accessibility tree within a live region is modified.

type "text" | "additions" | "additions removals" | "additions text" | "all" | "removals" | "removals additions" | "removals text" | "text additions" | "text removals"
optional

aria-required?

Indicates that user input is required on the element before a form may be submitted.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-roledescription?

Defines a human-readable, author-localized description for the role of an element.

type string
optional

aria-rowcount?

Defines the total number of rows in a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-rowindex?

Defines an element's row index or position with respect to the total number of rows within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-rowspan?

Defines the number of rows spanned by a cell or gridcell within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-selected?

Indicates the current "selected" state of various widgets.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-setsize?

Defines the number of items in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.

type number
optional

aria-sort?

Indicates if items in a table or grid are sorted in ascending or descending order.

type "none" | "ascending" | "descending" | "other"
optional

aria-valuemax?

Defines the maximum allowed value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuemin?

Defines the minimum allowed value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuenow?

Defines the current value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuetext?

Defines the human readable text alternative of aria-valuenow for a range widget.

type string
optional

children?

type Snippet<[]>
optional

type ClipboardEventHandler<HTMLDivElement> | ClipboardEventHandler<HTMLSpanElement>
optional

optional

optional

oncutcapture?

optional

on:paste?

type ClipboardEventHandler<HTMLDivElement> | ClipboardEventHandler<HTMLSpanElement>

mdz.ts

text

type string

mdz_parse_lexer
#

mdz_token_parser.ts

text

type string

mdz_text_content
#

mdz_helpers.ts

nodes

type MdzNode[]

mdz_to_svelte
#

mdz_to_svelte.ts

nodes

parsed mdz nodes to render

type MdzNode[]

components

Component name to import path mapping (e.g., {Alert: '$lib/Alert.svelte'}) If content references a component not in this map, has_unconfigured_tags is set.

type Record<string, string>

elements

Allowed HTML element names (e.g., new Set(['aside', 'details'])) If content references an element not in this set, has_unconfigured_tags is set.

type ReadonlySet<string>

base?

Base path for resolving relative links (e.g., '/docs/mdz/') When provided, relative references (./, ../) are resolved to absolute paths and passed through resolve(). Trailing slash recommended.

type string | undefined
optional

inputmode?

is?

Specify that a standard HTML element should behave like a defined custom built-in element

type string

bind:innerHTML?

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

type string
optional

bind:textContent?

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

optional

bind:innerText?

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

data-sveltekit-keepfocus?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-noscroll?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-preload-code?

type true | "" | "off" | "eager" | "viewport" | "hover" | "tap"
optional

data-sveltekit-preload-data?

type true | "" | "off" | "hover" | "tap"
optional

data-sveltekit-reload?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-replacestate?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

aria-activedescendant?

Identifies the currently active element when DOM focus is on a composite widget, textbox, group, or application.

type string
optional

aria-atomic?

Indicates whether assistive technologies will present all, or only parts of, the changed region based on the change notifications defined by the aria-relevant attribute.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-autocomplete?

Indicates whether inputting text could trigger display of one or more predictions of the user's intended value for an input and specifies how predictions would be presented if they are made.

type "none" | "list" | "inline" | "both"
optional

aria-busy?

Indicates an element is being modified and that assistive technologies MAY want to wait until the modifications are complete before exposing them to the user.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-checked?

Indicates the current "checked" state of checkboxes, radio buttons, and other widgets.

type boolean | "true" | "false" | "mixed"
optional

aria-colcount?

Defines the total number of columns in a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-colindex?

Defines an element's column index or position with respect to the total number of columns within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-colspan?

Defines the number of columns spanned by a cell or gridcell within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-controls?

Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current element.

type string
optional

aria-current?

Indicates the element that represents the current item within a container or set of related elements.

type Booleanish | "date" | "page" | "step" | "location" | "time"
optional

aria-describedby?

Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

type string
optional

aria-details?

Identifies the element that provides a detailed, extended description for the object.

type string
optional

aria-disabled?

Indicates that the element is perceivable but disabled, so it is not editable or otherwise operable.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-dropeffect?

Indicates what functions can be performed when a dragged object is released on the drop target.

type "none" | "link" | "copy" | "execute" | "move" | "popup"
optional

aria-errormessage?

Identifies the element that provides an error message for the object.

type string
optional

aria-expanded?

Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded or collapsed.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-flowto?

Identifies the next element (or elements) in an alternate reading order of content which, at the user's discretion, allows assistive technology to override the general default of reading in document source order.

type string
optional

aria-grabbed?

Indicates an element's "grabbed" state in a drag-and-drop operation.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-haspopup?

Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that can be triggered by an element.

type Booleanish | "dialog" | "grid" | "listbox" | "menu" | "tree"
optional

aria-hidden?

Indicates whether the element is exposed to an accessibility API.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-invalid?

Indicates the entered value does not conform to the format expected by the application.

type Booleanish | "grammar" | "spelling"
optional

aria-keyshortcuts?

Indicates keyboard shortcuts that an author has implemented to activate or give focus to an element.

type string
optional

aria-label?

Defines a string value that labels the current element.

type string
optional

aria-labelledby?

Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

type string
optional

aria-level?

Defines the hierarchical level of an element within a structure.

type number
optional

aria-live?

Indicates that an element will be updated, and describes the types of updates the user agents, assistive technologies, and user can expect from the live region.

type "off" | "assertive" | "polite"
optional

aria-modal?

Indicates whether an element is modal when displayed.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-multiline?

Indicates whether a text box accepts multiple lines of input or only a single line.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-multiselectable?

Indicates that the user may select more than one item from the current selectable descendants.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-orientation?

Indicates whether the element's orientation is horizontal, vertical, or unknown/ambiguous.

type "horizontal" | "vertical"
optional

aria-owns?

Identifies an element (or elements) in order to define a visual, functional, or contextual parent/child relationship between DOM elements where the DOM hierarchy cannot be used to represent the relationship.

type string
optional

aria-placeholder?

Defines a short hint (a word or short phrase) intended to aid the user with data entry when the control has no value. A hint could be a sample value or a brief description of the expected format.

type string
optional

aria-posinset?

Defines an element's number or position in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.

type number
optional

aria-pressed?

Indicates the current "pressed" state of toggle buttons.

type boolean | "true" | "false" | "mixed"
optional

aria-readonly?

Indicates that the element is not editable, but is otherwise operable.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-relevant?

Indicates what notifications the user agent will trigger when the accessibility tree within a live region is modified.

type "text" | "additions" | "additions removals" | "additions text" | "all" | "removals" | "removals additions" | "removals text" | "text additions" | "text removals"
optional

aria-required?

Indicates that user input is required on the element before a form may be submitted.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-roledescription?

Defines a human-readable, author-localized description for the role of an element.

type string
optional

aria-rowcount?

Defines the total number of rows in a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-rowindex?

Defines an element's row index or position with respect to the total number of rows within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-rowspan?

Defines the number of rows spanned by a cell or gridcell within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-selected?

Indicates the current "selected" state of various widgets.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-setsize?

Defines the number of items in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.

type number
optional

aria-sort?

Indicates if items in a table or grid are sorted in ascending or descending order.

type "none" | "ascending" | "descending" | "other"
optional

aria-valuemax?

Defines the maximum allowed value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuemin?

Defines the minimum allowed value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuenow?

Defines the current value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuetext?

Defines the human readable text alternative of aria-valuenow for a range widget.

type string
optional

children

Redirect
#

Redirect.svelte

host?

The target host to redirect to. Defaults to '' (relative URL).

type string
optional

path?

The target path to redirect to. Defaults to the current location.pathname.

type string
optional

auto?

Should the redirect happen automatically without user input? Defaults to true.

type boolean
optional

children?

type Snippet<[url: string]>
optional

register_section_header_context

(call)

get_fragment
type () => string

render_value_to_string
#

helpers.ts

value

type unknown

repo_name_parse
#

package_helpers.ts

name

package name (can be scoped like '@org/package')

type string

throws

  • Error - if scoped package name is malformed

examples

repo_name_parse('@fuzdev/fuz_ui') // => 'fuz_ui'
repo_name_parse('lodash') // => 'lodash'

repo_url_github_owner
#

package_helpers.ts

repo_url

repository URL (e.g., 'https://github.com/owner/repo')

type string

examples

repo_url_github_owner('https://github.com/fuzdev/fuz_ui') // => 'fuzdev'
repo_url_github_owner('https://gitlab.com/foo/bar') // => null (not a GitHub URL)

repo_url_parse
#

package_helpers.ts

repository

the repository field from package.json

type string | { [x: string]: unknown; type: string; url: string; directory?: string | undefined; } | undefined

examples

repo_url_parse('https://github.com/foo/bar') // => 'https://github.com/foo/bar'
repo_url_parse({url: 'git+https://github.com/foo/bar.git'}) // => 'https://github.com/foo/bar'
repo_url_parse(undefined) // => null

resolve_relative_path
#

mdz_helpers.ts

reference

a relative path starting with ./ or ../

type string

base

An absolute base path (e.g., '/docs/mdz/'). Empty string is treated as root.

type string

Teleport
#

Teleport.svelte

to?

Defaults to undefined to lessen friction with SSR. We may want to change this to optionally accept a string selector, but that didn't seem to be the best API for the Dialog.

type HTMLElement
optional

onmove?

type (el: HTMLElement, to: HTMLElement) => void
optional

results?

security?

unselectable?

type "off" | "on"
optional

inputmode?

Hints at the type of data that might be entered by the user while editing the element or its contents

type "none" | "search" | "text" | "tel" | "url" | "email" | "numeric" | "decimal"
optional

is?

Specify that a standard HTML element should behave like a defined custom built-in element

type string
optional

bind:innerHTML?

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

type string
optional

bind:textContent?

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

type string
optional

bind:innerText?

Elements with the contenteditable attribute support innerHTML, textContent and innerText bindings.

type string
optional

bind:focused?

type boolean
optional

bind:offsetWidth?

type number
optional

bind:offsetHeight?

type number
optional

data-sveltekit-keepfocus?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-noscroll?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-preload-code?

type true | "" | "off" | "eager" | "viewport" | "hover" | "tap"
optional

data-sveltekit-preload-data?

type true | "" | "off" | "hover" | "tap"
optional

data-sveltekit-reload?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

data-sveltekit-replacestate?

type true | "" | "off"
optional

aria-activedescendant?

Identifies the currently active element when DOM focus is on a composite widget, textbox, group, or application.

type string
optional

aria-atomic?

Indicates whether assistive technologies will present all, or only parts of, the changed region based on the change notifications defined by the aria-relevant attribute.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-autocomplete?

Indicates whether inputting text could trigger display of one or more predictions of the user's intended value for an input and specifies how predictions would be presented if they are made.

type "none" | "list" | "inline" | "both"
optional

aria-busy?

Indicates an element is being modified and that assistive technologies MAY want to wait until the modifications are complete before exposing them to the user.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-checked?

Indicates the current "checked" state of checkboxes, radio buttons, and other widgets.

type boolean | "true" | "false" | "mixed"
optional

aria-colcount?

Defines the total number of columns in a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-colindex?

Defines an element's column index or position with respect to the total number of columns within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-colspan?

Defines the number of columns spanned by a cell or gridcell within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-controls?

Identifies the element (or elements) whose contents or presence are controlled by the current element.

type string
optional

aria-current?

Indicates the element that represents the current item within a container or set of related elements.

type Booleanish | "date" | "page" | "step" | "location" | "time"
optional

aria-describedby?

Identifies the element (or elements) that describes the object.

type string
optional

aria-details?

Identifies the element that provides a detailed, extended description for the object.

type string
optional

aria-disabled?

Indicates that the element is perceivable but disabled, so it is not editable or otherwise operable.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-dropeffect?

Indicates what functions can be performed when a dragged object is released on the drop target.

type "none" | "link" | "copy" | "execute" | "move" | "popup"
optional

aria-errormessage?

Identifies the element that provides an error message for the object.

type string
optional

aria-expanded?

Indicates whether the element, or another grouping element it controls, is currently expanded or collapsed.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-flowto?

Identifies the next element (or elements) in an alternate reading order of content which, at the user's discretion, allows assistive technology to override the general default of reading in document source order.

type string
optional

aria-grabbed?

Indicates an element's "grabbed" state in a drag-and-drop operation.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-haspopup?

Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that can be triggered by an element.

type Booleanish | "dialog" | "grid" | "listbox" | "menu" | "tree"
optional

aria-hidden?

Indicates whether the element is exposed to an accessibility API.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-invalid?

Indicates the entered value does not conform to the format expected by the application.

type Booleanish | "grammar" | "spelling"
optional

aria-keyshortcuts?

Indicates keyboard shortcuts that an author has implemented to activate or give focus to an element.

type string
optional

aria-label?

Defines a string value that labels the current element.

type string
optional

aria-labelledby?

Identifies the element (or elements) that labels the current element.

type string
optional

aria-level?

Defines the hierarchical level of an element within a structure.

type number
optional

aria-live?

Indicates that an element will be updated, and describes the types of updates the user agents, assistive technologies, and user can expect from the live region.

type "off" | "assertive" | "polite"
optional

aria-modal?

Indicates whether an element is modal when displayed.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-multiline?

Indicates whether a text box accepts multiple lines of input or only a single line.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-multiselectable?

Indicates that the user may select more than one item from the current selectable descendants.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-orientation?

Indicates whether the element's orientation is horizontal, vertical, or unknown/ambiguous.

type "horizontal" | "vertical"
optional

aria-owns?

Identifies an element (or elements) in order to define a visual, functional, or contextual parent/child relationship between DOM elements where the DOM hierarchy cannot be used to represent the relationship.

type string
optional

aria-placeholder?

Defines a short hint (a word or short phrase) intended to aid the user with data entry when the control has no value. A hint could be a sample value or a brief description of the expected format.

type string
optional

aria-posinset?

Defines an element's number or position in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.

type number
optional

aria-pressed?

Indicates the current "pressed" state of toggle buttons.

type boolean | "true" | "false" | "mixed"
optional

aria-readonly?

Indicates that the element is not editable, but is otherwise operable.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-relevant?

Indicates what notifications the user agent will trigger when the accessibility tree within a live region is modified.

type "text" | "additions" | "additions removals" | "additions text" | "all" | "removals" | "removals additions" | "removals text" | "text additions" | "text removals"
optional

aria-required?

Indicates that user input is required on the element before a form may be submitted.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-roledescription?

Defines a human-readable, author-localized description for the role of an element.

type string
optional

aria-rowcount?

Defines the total number of rows in a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-rowindex?

Defines an element's row index or position with respect to the total number of rows within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-rowspan?

Defines the number of rows spanned by a cell or gridcell within a table, grid, or treegrid.

type number
optional

aria-selected?

Indicates the current "selected" state of various widgets.

type Booleanish
optional

aria-setsize?

Defines the number of items in the current set of listitems or treeitems. Not required if all elements in the set are present in the DOM.

type number
optional

aria-sort?

Indicates if items in a table or grid are sorted in ascending or descending order.

type "none" | "ascending" | "descending" | "other"
optional

aria-valuemax?

Defines the maximum allowed value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuemin?

Defines the minimum allowed value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuenow?

Defines the current value for a range widget.

type number
optional

aria-valuetext?

Defines the human readable text alternative of aria-valuenow for a range widget.

type string
optional

children?

type Snippet<[]>
optional

TomeLink
#

TomeLink.svelte

name

type string

docs_path?

type string
optional

hash?

URL fragment to append, with or without the #.

type string
optional

tomes_context
#

tome.ts

TomeSection
#

TomeSection.svelte

children

type Snippet<[]>

TomeSectionHeader
#

TomeSectionHeader.svelte

children?

type Snippet<[]>
optional

text

type string

tag?

type DocsLinkTag
optional

trim_trailing_punctuation
#

mdz_helpers.ts

url

type string

tsdoc_see_to_mdz
#

tsdoc_mdz.ts

content

raw @see tag content in TSDoc format

type string

examples

tsdoc_see_to_mdz('://fuz.dev|API Docs') // → '[API Docs](https://fuz.dev)' tsdoc_see_to_mdz('') // → '`SomeType`' tsdoc_see_to_mdz('https://example.com') // → 'https://example.com' tsdoc_see_to_mdz('library_gen.ts for Gro-specific integration') // → '`library_gen.ts` for Gro-specific integration'

TypeLink
#

TypeLink.svelte

type

type string

hash?

URL fragment to append, with or without the #.

type string
optional

UNDERSCORE
#

mdz_helpers.ts

url_api_declaration
#

library_helpers.ts

declaration_name

name of the declaration to link to

type string

url_api_declaration_full
#

library_helpers.ts

homepage

package homepage URL

type string

declaration_name

name of the declaration to link to

type string

url_api_module
#

library_helpers.ts

module_path

module path (e.g., 'helpers.ts')

type string

url_github_file
#

package_helpers.ts

repo_url

repository URL (e.g., 'https://github.com/owner/repo')

type string

file_path

path to the file (leading './' is stripped)

type string

line?

optional line number for deep linking

type number | undefined
optional

examples

url_github_file('https://github.com/foo/bar', 'src/index.ts') // => 'https://github.com/foo/bar/blob/main/src/index.ts'
url_github_file('https://github.com/foo/bar', './src/index.ts', 42) // => 'https://github.com/foo/bar/blob/main/src/index.ts#L42'

url_github_org
#

package_helpers.ts

repo_url

repository URL (e.g., 'https://github.com/owner/repo')

type string

repo_name

repository name to strip from the URL

type string

examples

url_github_org('https://github.com/fuzdev/fuz_ui', 'fuz_ui') // => 'https://github.com/fuzdev'

url_npm_package
#

package_helpers.ts

package_name

package name (can be scoped like '@org/package')

type string

examples

url_npm_package('@fuzdev/fuz_ui') // => 'https://www.npmjs.com/package/@fuzdev/fuz_ui'

url_package_logo
#

library_helpers.ts

homepage_url

package homepage URL, or null

type string | null

logo_path?

optional custom logo path (defaults to 'favicon.png')

type string | undefined
optional

url_to_root_relative
#

library_helpers.ts

url

full URL to convert

type string

origin

origin to strip (defaults to current page origin)

type string

examples

// Assuming page.url.origin is 'https://example.com' url_to_root_relative('https://example.com/docs/api') // => '/docs/api'

url_well_known
#

package_helpers.ts

homepage_url

package homepage URL

type string

filename

filename in .well-known directory

type string

examples

url_well_known('https://fuz.dev', 'package.json') // => 'https://fuz.dev/.well-known/package.json'

vite_plugin_library_well_known
#

vite_plugin_library_well_known.ts

options

type VitePluginLibraryWellKnownOptions

examples

// vite.config.ts import {defineConfig} from 'vite'; import {sveltekit} from '@sveltejs/kit/vite'; import {vite_plugin_library_well_known} from '@fuzdev/fuz_ui/vite_plugin_library_well_known.js'; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [sveltekit(), vite_plugin_library_well_known()], });

VitePluginLibraryWellKnownOptions
#

vite_plugin_library_well_known.ts

library_path

Z_LOWER
#

mdz_helpers.ts

Z_UPPER
#

mdz_helpers.ts

ZERO
#

mdz_helpers.ts

📜/docs/api/fuz_ui
fuz.dev
